steps.title: Multiply both sides by the LCD to clear all fractions
Test the candidate answer by substituting back
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice C (−19): Substitute $x = -19$ into the original equation:

$$\frac{1}{c(-19)} = \frac{-19}{76} + \frac{1}{c}$$

Simplify the left side:
$$-\frac{1}{19c} = \frac{-19}{76} + \frac{1}{c}$$

Multiply both sides by $19c$ to solve for $c$:
$$-1 = \frac{-361c}{76} + 19$$

$$-20 = \frac{-361c}{76}$$

$$1520 = 361c$$

$$c = \frac{1520}{361} = \frac{80}{19}$$

Since a valid value of $c$ exists (namely $\frac{80}{19}$) that satisfies the equation when $x = -19$, the substitution succeeds. That matches choice C — C is correct.
